tangential projector and geodesic distance from Mathlib

Atomless decomposition (Sierpinski/Lyapunov splitting): an atomless probability measure splits into M probability pieces with prescribed positive convex weights and pairwise disjoint supports (mu = sum alpha_k P_k). MACHINE-CHECKED (fully de-axiomatised): the pieces are normalized restrictions (alpha_k)^-1 mu.restrict(A_k) to a prescribed-mass disjoint partition carved by iterating the now-proved Sierpinski IVT primitive (sierpinski-ivt); the partition and probability-normalization scaffolding is machine-checked (Foundations.exists_probability_decomposition). Positive weights (alpha_k != 0) are assumed so each piece is a genuine probability measure. #print axioms lists only propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound. Soundness fix: the earlier per-piece hemisphere clause was inconsistent at M=1 and was dropped; prop_2_2 now acquires the hemisphere dynamically per piece via lemma_3_2.

Disentanglement dynamics output (Section 3.3): one schedule concentrates each measure into a small ball B(alpha_i, r), r<1, around a unit direction alpha_i, with directions pairwise 2r-separated. The concrete geometric output of Lemmas 3.2-3.4 run as the Section 3.3 induction (leaf L11 the geometric core); rests on the missing continuity-equation theory.

Structural flow algebra (Axioms/Dynamics.lean): schedule composition comp with measureFlow_comp/flowMap_comp, switch sub-additivity switches_comp, the identity schedule idParams, time-reversal inv with measureFlow_inv, and the pushforward identity measureFlow_map (Phi_theta^t mu = (Phi_theta^t)_# mu). Standard continuity-equation semigroup facts, absent from Mathlib; used to assemble the construction.

Parking / simultaneous action (Appendix B): if a family of measures has pairwise disjoint supports and each member can be steered to within eps of its target by some schedule, a single schedule steers all of them simultaneously (each gated to its region, parking on the others).

Convexity of W2 under mixtures: for probability measures with shared weights, W2(sum a_k P_k, sum a_k Q_k) is at most the uniform bound on the component distances (gluing of couplings, W2(mix)^2 <= sum a_k W2(P_k,Q_k)^2). Standard optimal-transport fact, absent from Mathlib.

Single-measure controllability (Prop 2.1 + Prop 4.1): a probability measure in an open hemisphere can be driven W2-close to the Dirac mass at any prescribed point, by clustering then steering the cluster. Lifted to a family by disentanglement and parking in Theorem 1.1.

E1: integrate the ReLU-gated flow and verify mass fraction in B0 cap B1 >= 1-eps, and >= (1-eps)^K through a K-ball chain. Tests Lemma B.2 / B.1.
E2: simulate the attention-field flow and verify diam(conv_g supp mu(t)) -> 0 with rate ~ log(1/eps). Tests Proposition 2.1.
E3: run theta_1 on N overlapping-support measures and verify supports become pairwise disjoint; count switches ~ O(dN). Tests Proposition 3.1.
E4: run gather/corridor/restore and verify x_0^M -> y^M while inactive points stay fixed; switches <= 6M. Tests Proposition 4.2 / 4.1.
E5: simulate the d=2 dynamics and verify E = 1 - cos theta decreases monotonically and x_i -> u_i. Tests Example 6.1.
E6: compose disentangle/match/un-disentangle on small N, M and verify W2(mu(T), target) <= eps with switch count O((d+M)N) / O(dN). Tests Theorems 1.1 / 1.2.
E7 (negative control): show a single linear continuity equation cannot match overlapping inputs to disjoint targets (eq. 1.7).

Geodesic convexity on the sphere (milestone M5, foundations). MACHINE-CHECKED. A subset s of the unit sphere S^{d-1} is geodesically convex (GeodesicConvex) when it is closed under normalized positive chords: for x,y in s and a,b>0, the point ||a*x+b*y||^-1 * (a*x+b*y) lies in s. On an open hemisphere these normalized chords are exactly the minimizing geodesic arcs, so this is the faithful spherical analogue of ordinary convexity (pure inner-product characterization, no arccos). Key lemma geodesicConvex_open_hemisphere: an open spherical hemisphere {x in S | 0 < <e,x>} is geodesically convex -- the fact the paper uses for the orthant/hemisphere confinement (Lemma 3.2). Also: singletons are geodesically convex, and geodesic convexity is closed under arbitrary (nonempty-indexed) intersection, so an orthant (intersection of hemispheres) is geodesically convex. Mathlib has Convex/ConvexCone/SameRay but no geodesic convexity, so this is built in-repo. #print axioms clean. First slice toward the disentanglement geometry behind exists_disentangling_balls; generalizes leaf L11 (barycenter_noncolinear_of_disjoint_hull).

The geodesic hull is geodesically convex (milestone M5, hull bridge). MACHINE-CHECKED. Connects leaf L11's geodesicHull s = cone(s) cap S^{d-1} to the GeodesicConvex predicate: geodesicConvex_geodesicHull -- if every generator of s lies strictly on e's side (0 < <e,p>, i.e. s is in the open hemisphere of e), then geodesicHull s is geodesically convex. This is the 'hull = cone cap sphere is geodesic-convex' characterization the paper asserts when it calls conv_g geodesic-convex. Also geodesicHull_subset_hemisphere: the hull sits inside the open hemisphere of e. Together with geodesicConvex_open_hemisphere this gives the exact geometric picture behind Section 3.3 disentanglement (disjoint hulls inside a common hemisphere). Supporting facts inConicalSpan.add (cone closed under addition) and inner_pos_of_inConicalSpan (a nonzero conical point in e's hemisphere has 0 < <e,.>) proved too. #print axioms clean.

The geodesic hull is the smallest geodesically convex set (milestone M5, minimality). MACHINE-CHECKED. geodesicHull_subset_of_geodesicConvex: if C is geodesically convex and contains every point of s, then geodesicHull s subset of C. This is the universal property that justifies calling geodesicHull a hull. Proof by Finset.induction (normalize_conical_mem): split off one generator p0; the remaining sub-combination u, once nonzero, normalizes into C by the inductive hypothesis, and normalize(w*p0 + u) = normalize(w*p0 + ||u||*normalize(u)) is a normalized positive chord of p0 in C and normalize(u) in C, hence in C by geodesic convexity. Companions: geodesicHull_mono (monotone in the generating set), mem_geodesicHull_self (each unit generator is in its hull), inConicalSpan.mono. #print axioms clean. Completes the hull characterization side of M5 (hull = smallest geodesic-convex set = cone cap sphere).
L9: if each step multiplies retained mass by at least (1-eps), then after K steps mu(T, B_K) >= (1-eps)^K mu_0(union B_k) (Lemma B.1 induction, abstracted).

L11: empirical measures in the orthant (geodesic hull modeled as cone(s) cap sphere) with disjoint geodesic hulls have non-colinear barycenters (not SameRay). Discharges review finding F2: the implication Prop 3.1's induction asserts without proof, for the empirical regime of Thm 1.1 and restricted Thm 1.2. General-measure case adds only 'barycenter in closed convex hull of support'.

L11': finding F2 for arbitrary probability measures. Two probability measures supported in disjoint closed convex cones have non-colinear (not SameRay) barycenters, via Mathlib's Convex.integral_mem (barycenter in any closed convex set carrying full mass). Closes the general-measure residual of F2 with no new axioms.
L6: along d/dt <x,alpha> = c (1 - <alpha,x>^2) with c != 0 of fixed sign, the inner product is monotone and converges to +-1 (sign of c), i.e. x -> +-alpha (eq. B.9).
L7: the map-induced coupling gives W2^2(T1_# mu, T2_# mu) <= integral ||T1 - T2||^2 d mu (the core of Lemma 5.2).
L2: with U=-1 z^T, b=cos(R) 1, W 1 = omega, the ReLU gate equals (cos R - <z,x>)_+ and d/dt <x,omega> = (1 - <x,omega>^2)(cos R - cos d_g(z,x))_+ (eq. B.4-B.5).
L4: the Riemannian gradient of geodesic distance is grad_1 d_g(x, omega) = - P_x^perp omega / sqrt(1 - <x,omega>^2).
L5: for E(theta) = 1 - cos theta along theta' = -alpha sin theta, the derivative is E' = -alpha sin^2 theta <= 0 (Example 6.1 Lyapunov function).
L8: if W2(mu, delta_{x0}) <= eta2 then 1 - mu(B(x0, eta3)) <= C eta2 / eta3, via a Lipschitz bump and W1 duality (Claim 2).
L10: a function that is constant on an open ball forces the stated barycenter identity to fail, so some x* in B makes the two barycenters differ (Lemma 3.4 Part 1).
L1: the tangential projector P_x^perp = I - x x^T is symmetric, idempotent, kills x, and satisfies <P_x^perp v, v> = ||v||^2 - <x,v>^2 for ||x||=1.
L3: monotonicity of cos gives <omega, x_0^M> - cos(pi/8 + tau) < 0 when d_g(omega, x_0^M) >= pi/2 and tau in (0, 3pi/8) (Prop 4.2 Step 1 separating hyperplane).
record Phase 7 closure, axiom surface, and refreshed ClaimGraph
Lemma 3.2: one switch of W transports measures not filling the sphere into the positive orthant cap Q_1^{d-1}.
Lemma 3.3: if barycenters are pairwise non-colinear, a piecewise-constant theta shrinks one measure's geodesic convex hull into a small ball around its barycenter direction.
Lemma 3.4: two measures can be made non-colinear in barycenter, via the perceptron (gamma1=1) or via attention (gamma1!=1).
Lemma 5.1: transport maps T^i are preserved by the disentangling flow maps; there is a Lipschitz invertible psi with the stated pushforward identity.
Lemma 5.2 (linearized OT bound): W2(T1_# mu, T2_# mu) <= ||T1 - T2||_{L2(mu)} for measurable T1, T2 with T1 bijective.
Lemma 5.4: any psi in L2(S^{d-1}; S^{d-1}) is approximated in L2(mu) by a flow map of the continuity equation with finitely many switches.
Lemma A.1: the support condition (1.5) can be removed at O(eta) cost in W2 by a curated input perturbation.

Lemma B.1: through a chain of K+1 consecutively overlapping balls, K switches retain mass mu(T, B_K) >= (1-eps)^K mu(B_0) (the mass starting in the first ball, funneled along the chain). PROVED by induction over Lemma B.2 + the structural flow algebra (the only axioms). Statement corrected from the stub's mu(union B_k), which makes K=0 false.
Lemma B.2: through a single overlapping ball pair, mass mu(T, B0 cap B1) >= (1-eps) mu_0(B0), via the ReLU-gated drift toward an interior anchor.

The labeled axiom layer: W2/W1 with the coupling and KR-duality bounds, and the continuity-equation flow map with its Lipschitz/bijective/parked-identity properties, standing in for the missing Mathlib theory.

Mathlib v4.31.0 coverage of the prerequisites: optimal transport / W2, continuity-equation flows, geodesic convexity, LaSalle and Hartman-Grobman are absent; sphere, inner-product algebra, and ODE existence/Gronwall are present.
Proposition 2.1: attention with parameter B clusters a single-hemisphere measure to a point mass; diam(conv_g supp mu(t)) -> 0 with rate O(log 1/eps).

Proposition 2.2: an atomless probability measure can be driven W2-close to a prescribed M-atom discrete measure (convex weights). PROVED (needs 0<d, positive weights alpha_k != 0): partition into probability pieces of mass alpha_k with pairwise disjoint supports (exists_atomless_partition); per piece rotate into the orthant with one switch (lemma_3_2), which lies in a basis direction's open hemisphere, then cluster to its target point (cluster_to_point) composing the schedules; run all pieces with one parked schedule (exists_parked_schedule), then lift per-piece bounds via the convexity of W2 under mixtures (W2_convexCombo_le); the convex-combination bookkeeping is machine-checked.

Proposition 3.1: a piecewise-constant theta disentangles the supports of N measures (pairwise disjoint, each in a hemisphere). PROVED from exists_disentangling_balls: the disjointness + hemisphere packaging is machine-checked (Metric.ball_disjoint_ball + Cauchy-Schwarz), closing the F2 hand-wave; the dynamical construction is the more primitive axiom.

Proposition 4.1: M distinct points x_0^i can be steered exactly to distinct y^i, using at most 6M switches. PROVED by induction on M over Proposition 4.2 and the structural flow algebra (idParams base case; comp/switches_comp step placing one more point; flowMap_comp); the 6M switch budget is machine-checked.

Proposition 4.2: with distinct inputs/targets and the first M-1 points already at their targets, the single active point x_0^M is steered to y^M while the inactive points stay fixed, via gather/corridor/restore, with at most 6 switches. (Injectivity hypotheses added for soundness: the flow is bijective.)
Proposition 6.2: for generic i.i.d. discrete inputs, a single constant parameter disentangles all measures almost surely (limit clusters are distinct).
data-driven experiment validation report (HTML + PDF)
adversarial proof-review of the mid-level lemmas

Sierpinski's intermediate-value theorem for nonatomic measures (subset form). MACHINE-CHECKED (was an axiom, now fully proved). Real-line case (exists_measurableSet_subset_measure_eq_real): for a finite atomless measure mu on R, a measurable set E, and any target r <= mu E, there is a measurable subset F of E with mu F = r -- proved directly, the crux being that t |-> (mu(E cap Iic t)).toReal is continuous because its increment over [0,t] is the Bochner primitive int_0^t 1_E dmu (intervalIntegral.continuous_primitive, holds precisely because mu has NoAtoms), so it runs from 0 (t -> -inf, tendsto_measure_iInter_atBot) to (mu E).toReal (t -> +inf, tendsto_measure_iUnion_atTop) and the intermediate value theorem (intermediate_value_Icc) attains r.toReal at some t0; take F = E cap Iic t0. Standard-Borel case (exists_measurableSet_subset_measure_eq) reduces to the R-case by pushing mu forward along the measurable embedding embeddingReal into R (injective, so the pushforward stays finite and atomless), solving there, and pulling the subset back. Both #print axioms clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound). Classical theorem (Sierpinski 1922; Fremlin, Measure Theory Vol.2, 215D), previously absent from Mathlib. Soundness note: NoAtoms alone is insufficient on a coarse sigma-algebra (countable-cocountable 0/1 counterexample); the standard-Borel hypothesis is exactly what supplies the missing richness.
assemble the rich Quarto website into /docs for classic Pages
Theorem 1.1: for Dirac targets sharing a missing direction, a single Transformer steers N inputs to within eps in W2, with O(d N) switches and norm O(dN/T + log 1/eps).
Theorem 1.2: for targets matchable by transport maps and a shared missing direction, a single Transformer steers N inputs to within eps in W2 with piecewise-constant parameters.
